snippet.title: Semiconductors and National Security: The CHIPS Challenge: Aspen Security Forum 2023
snippet.description:
Semiconductors and computer chips are essential to modern technology and defense capabilities. As relations with China continue to grow more tense, the need to enhance
U.S. semiconductor manufacturing capabilities becomes increasingly important. Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger and Former U.S. Secretary of Commerce Penny Pritzker broadly discuss the semiconductor industry’s critical role in national security, including supply chain concerns and efforts to ensure the industry’s competitiveness.


Speakers:

Pat Gelsinger, CEO, Intel

Penny Pritzker, Founder and Chairman, PSP Partners, and 38th U.S. Secretary of Commerce

Moderator: Steve Clemons, Founding Editor at Large, Semafor

snippet.title: Intel's CEO Shares His Plan To Win The AI Chip War — With Pat Gelsinger
snippet.description:
Pat Gelsinger is the CEO of Intel. He joins Big Technology Podcast to discuss how the AI chip war is playing out and why it's growing more competitive. 

Listen to the full interview, and others like it, on your podcast app of choice:

Spotify:  https://spoti.fi/32aZGZx
Apple:  https://apple.co/3AebxCK
Etc. https://pod.link/1522960417/ 

In this episode, we break down the various components of the chip business, the difference between CPUs and GPUs, who's ahead in the AI chip war, NVIDIA's underrated weakness, and why Intel is getting back into the foundry business after a couple of failed attempts. Tune in for a vibrant, deep discussion of the state of the AI chip business and the state of Intel's comeback.

snippet.title: Intel CEO's Vision and Analysis of Asian Countries' Capabilities
snippet.description:
intel CEO Pat Gelsinger expressed at the DAVOS Forum on January 17th that he hopes the global chip market will reduce its dependence on Taiwan. He also believes that this will help the world economy. As the leading chip manufacturer in the US, it is not difficult to understand Gelsinger's expectations. In an interview with CNBC in May of last year, Gelsinger emphasized that the world will "never" be completely independent of Taiwan, but he also expected that ten years from now, the chip production capacity of the US, EU, and Asia will be evenly divided. However, the EU and the US together currently account for only 20%, while China, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan in Asia account for 80%. Gelsinger's vision of "half and half" has not been recognized by leaders of other Asian countries.

snippet.title: Intel CEO: 'Disappointed' Tower deal had to be scrapped
snippet.description:
Intel (INTC) scrapped its acquisition of Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) after Chinese regulators failed to approve the deal in a timely manner. In an interview with Yahoo Finance's Akiko Fujita, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger says the company was "disappointed" the deal couldn't happen. Gelsinger says the deal would have "accelerated" his ambitions for the company to be a foundry, but now he will "have to find other ways to drive the agenda because it's not likely that any major technology acquisition will get regulatory approval." It's something Gelsinger says is "disappointing because M&A has always been a factor in how technology companies have been building and moving their strategies forward."

snippet.title: Getting the Geek Back: Innovation is for Developers — with Intel's Pat Gelsinger
snippet.description:
On this episode of The Six Five On The Road, sponsored by Intel, hosts Daniel Newman and Patrick Moorhead welcome Intel’s CEO, Pat Gelsinger for a conversation on the “Siliconomy” and how Intel is “Getting the Geek Back” by reinforcing their belief that InnovatiON is for developers, as well as the opportunities created by the generational shift AI.

Their discussion covers:

* What priorities CEO, Pat Gelsinger has been razor-focused on during his three-year tenure at Intel

* How Intel’s AI approach goes where others aren’t yet–AI on the PC, at the Edge, in the Enterprise, as well as the cloud and data center with a broader range of hardware and software

* An expanded look at Pat’s keynote highlights, including the new coined phrase “Siliconomy” 

* The main takeaways Intel hopes to deliver to developers over the course of the event

snippet.title: Intel CEO says 'the worst is behind us'
snippet.description:
#intel #yahoofinance #intelceo #ai #q3 

After posting two straight quarters of losses, Intel (INTC) returned to profitability in the second quarter. In an interview with Yahoo Finance Live, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger says he thinks "the worst is behind us at this point and our raise to the guide for Q3 really portends to a more optimistic future." One of the big issues for Intel has been the slowdown in PC demand. On that Gelsinger says the company is "expecting nice quarter-on-quarter improvements" in that business, partly due to inventory adjustments. When it comes to AI, Gelsinger explains there are a couple of reasons why Intel's name isn't mentioned much in the conversation. One of the reasons Gelsinger gives is that "we haven't been as aggressive out of the chute as we should've been." Gelsinger says the company has to prove itself because "we have a clear market leader who is executing very well." Gelsinger says rival Nvidia (NVDA) "has done a good job in this space. They've really led the way and I like to say, they worked super hard and they got lucky." However, Gelsinger notes the AI space is still "fairly young" and there is still "a lot of opportunity" for Intel.

snippet.title: Nvidia Stock Hits $1 Trillion, Intel CEO says Nvidia's AI dominance is Pure Luck.
snippet.description:
#intel  ,#chips, #nvidia, #ai #nvidiastock 
Nvidia Stock Hits $1 Trillion, Intel CEO says Nvidia's AI dominance is Pure Luck. 

Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has publicly characterized Nvidia’s AI industry success as being purely accidental. In a wide-ranging interview with Gelsinger, hosted by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), the Intel boss told attendees that Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang “got extraordinarily lucky.”

Nvidia’s VP of Applied Deep Learning Research, Bryan Catanzaro, issued a concise dismissal of Gelsinger’s assertions about Nvidia benefitting from simple luck on X. Nvidia VP explain why he thinks “Nvidia’s dominance didn't come from luck.” Rather, he insists, “It came from vision and execution. Which Intel lacked.”

snippet.title: Intel's 'momentum is building' after topping quarter estimates: CEO
snippet.description:
Intel beat estimates in the latest quarter, surpassing expectations on both revenue and earnings while also raising guidance. Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger acknowledges there were some headwinds Intel was facing when he took the job, and his goal was to "rebuild this iconic company." With the latest results, he says "people can see the momentum is building" at Intel thanks to cost-saving initiatives, product execution, and its internal foundry model.
Intel plans to drop new products including the Core Ultra processor, which Gelsinger nicknamed the "AI PC," and Generations 4 and 5 of the Xeon product line. The AI PC aims to keep users from having to go to the cloud to utilize AI; instead, the features and data are readily available on the device itself to improve productivity. Gelsinger says these products will help Intel capitalize on the "surge in AI interest we're seeing across the board." 
With new products slated to launch over the next two years, Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger believes the chipmaker can regain lost market share and boost competitiveness, stating 2024 is when Intel will "start gaining back share" after its upcoming product releases.
"Intel is back in the data center, winning share, better products, better execution," Gelsinger tells Yahoo Finance's Executive Editor Brian Sozzi, adding: "And our customers are starting to see us and give us credit for that."

snippet.description:
米インテルが理化学研究所と提携し、量子コンピューター技術などの共同研究に乗り出します。パット・ゲルシンガー最高経営責任者（CEO）が都内で日本経済新聞とテレビ東京の単独取材に応じ、明らかにしました。
【記事】https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXZQOUC187C10Y3A510C2000000/

【日経電子版映像ページ】 http://www.nikkei.com/video/
※日本経済新聞社の動画について改ざんや、許可なく商用・営利 目的で利用することを禁じます。
＃インテル　＃理研　＃量子計算機

snippet.title: Intel Innovation 2023 Livestream - Day 2 featuring Greg Lavender Keynote
snippet.description:
Join us for Intel Innovation 2023, a conversation with developers, technologists, and engineers advancing technology for the future. See live demos from the show floor showcasing the latest achievements in AI, edge to cloud, client, and next gen platforms. to get a first look at solutions to  – the geek is back!

Agenda:
- 9 AM: Innovation Live - Join hosts Madeline Littrell and Tony Mongkolsmai for conversations with Arun Gupta and others. 
- 9:30 AM: Greg Lavender Keynote - Accelerating the Convergence of AI and Security
- 10:30 AM: Innovation Live - Continue the conversation and discover more solutions to drive innovation. CEO Pat Gelsinger joins for an interview. 

Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) are truly transforming the world. Together, we're working towards creating accessible and cost-effective training and inference platforms that empower you to make choices freely. This opens up incredible opportunities for developers, offering them the chance to push the boundaries of what's possible, tackle some of our planet's most significant challenges, and enhance the lives of people worldwide.

At Innovation 2023, we'll delve deep into these trends, providing developers, engineers, and technologists with a comprehensive understanding of our technology roadmap. Together, we'll explore how we can democratize AI across various domains, from client and edge computing to cloud solutions, utilizing next-generation systems and cutting-edge technology.

Our foundation is built on principles of openness, choice, and trust. We aim to equip developers with the confidence to harness the world's ever-growing demand for processing power, making AI more accessible and transparent for everyone. With Intel as your partner, you'll be well-prepared to seize the limitless potential of AI.
